title: MEDIUM: No string length caps on remote fields before DB insert
description:
**Category: Input Validation (6)**

Remote-controlled string fields are inserted into SQLite TEXT columns with no length cap:

1. **follower actor_uri** (server.rs:953) — attacker sends a Follow with a multi-megabyte actor URI. The 256KB body limit caps the total JSON, but individual fields can be nearly that large.
2. **follower inbox_uri** (server.rs:954) — same issue.
3. **Card title, description, site_name** (card.rs:180-195) — fetched from remote OG meta tags. The 100KB scan limit and 1MB body limit provide loose caps, but a single `og:title` could be 100KB.

**Impact:** Resource abuse / DB bloat. An attacker sending many Follow requests with near-max-length URIs can grow the database disproportionately. Card metadata from malicious pages can similarly bloat storage.

**Fix:** Add explicit per-field length caps before INSERT:
- actor_uri, inbox_uri: 2048 bytes (practical URI limit)
- Card title: 512 bytes
- Card description: 2048 bytes  
- Card site_name: 256 bytes

Truncate at UTF-8 boundary (the `truncate_utf8` helper in feed.rs already exists).
close_reason: Added length caps: follower actor_uri/inbox_uri reject >2048 bytes (server.rs). Card title capped at 512, description at 2048, site_name at 256 via truncate_utf8 (card.rs).
